data.title: Gilead opts out of deal for Arcus cancer therapy
data.description:
Shares of Arcus Biosciences (NYSE:RCUS) hit a new 52-week low on Tuesday after the biotech said that Gilead Sciences’ (NASDAQ:GILD) option rights to partner with the company for a potential anticancer agent called casdatifan have expired.

Hayward, California-headquartered Arcus (NYSE:RCUS) noted that, as a result, the company retains rights to the therapy, which was subject to a 10-year partnership initiated between the company and Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) in 2020.

Casdatifan is a small molecule designed to target the cancer-causing agent HIF-2a. Noting a recent data readout at a medical conference and possibly with Merck’s (MRK) Welireg in mind, RCUS CEO Terry Rosen highlighted casdatifan’s potential to become “a best-in-class HIF-2a inhibitor in what appears to be a two-horse race.”

On Tuesday, the New Jersey-based pharma giant announced the EU approval for its oral HIF-2a inhibitor as a single agent for two cancer conditions linked to kidneys.

“We are thrilled to retain ownership of casdatifan, which has the potential to address a significant unmet need for patients with an estimated $5 billion market opportunity,” Rosen added.

data.title: Capital One, Discover shareholders approve deal
data.description:
Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF) and Discover Financial Services (NYSE:DFS) holders approved the planned $35 billion purchase of Discover.

The shareholders of each company approved the deal in separate votes on Tuesday, according to a statement.

Capital One (NYSE:COF) expects the deal will close early this year, subject to approval by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, and other customary closing conditions.

data.title: Beyond Meat jumps to its 2025 high amid interest in its seared steak tips product
data.description:
Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) was one of the biggest gainers in the consumer sector on Tuesday, with a gain of 11.2%.

The company did not release any press releases or SEC filings to account for the buying action. However, Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) was profiled favorably in US Magazine in an article titled "Kim Kardashian and A-List Athletes Are Obsessed with This Plant-Based Meat Alternative." The publication highlighted that Beyond Seared Steak Tips are particularly popular with the jet set.

Google Search trends also confirm a recent spike in consumer interest in the number of queries related to Beyond Meat's Seared Steak Tips.

The rally pushed Beyond Meat (BYND) to its highest trading level of the year at $4.39 a share earlier in the session. However, the plant-based meat stock is still far below the 52-week high of $12.12 or the all-time high of $234.90 in 2019. Short interest on Beyond Meat (BYND) stands at 48% of the total float.

data.title: Silver Lake nears deal for stake in Intel's Altera
data.description:
Private equity firm Silver Lake Management is in exclusive discussions to purchase a majority stake in Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) Altera unit.

The PE firm is working out details of an investment in the Altera programmable chip unit, according to a Bloomerg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The exact size of the stake hasn't been decided.

Intel and Silver Lake declined to comment to Bloomberg.

Shares of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) jumped 14% on Tuesday after reports that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Broadcom (AVGO) could each look to acquire parts of the company.

data.title: CNX Resources raised at Raymond James as 'rising tide lifts all boats'
data.description:
CNX Resources (NYSE:CNX) +1.8% in Tuesday's trading as Raymond James upgrades to Market Perform from Underperform, believing the company will benefit from the "rising tide lifts all boats theme," assuming the firm's bullish natural gas outlook proves accurate.

Ray Jay analyst John Freeman says his biggest issue with CNX Resources (NYSE:CNX) is the company's hedges limit its ability to participate in the bullish natural gas outlook, but if the firm's strong outlook proves accurate, he believes the company will benefit from the "rising tide lifts all boats" theme.

The company admittedly will lag its gassy peers with more exposure to the strip, but Freeman says the stock no longer warrants an outright sell rating, anticipating an in-line performance compared to the broader market as its distributable cash flow gets dragged higher by an improving gas strip.

CNX (CNX) has lagged its gassy peers by ~40% over the past three months since natural gas prices began their big rally, with some of the sizzle taken out of the stock from the New Technologies segment following the 45V ruling at the start of the year along with the onerous hedge position, Freeman adds.

data.title: IHG targets 4%+ net system growth and launches $900M buyback for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: InterContinental Hotels Group PLC (NYSE:IHG) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Elie Maalouf highlighted 2024 as a year of strong financial performance, emphasizing 4.3% net system growth and a $900 million share buyback program for 2025. The company also finalized the acquisition of the Ruby brand for $116 million, expanding its presence in the premium urban lifestyle segment.
  • IHG added 59,000 rooms to its portfolio, taking the total to 987,000 across more than 6,600 hotels. Signings reached 106,000 rooms, increasing the pipeline to over 2,200 hotels.
  • CFO Michael Glover reported fee business revenue growth of 6% and a 190-basis-point increase in fee margin to 61.2%. Adjusted EPS grew 15%, supported by the $800 million share buyback completed in 2024.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its 2025 target of over 4% net system growth, excluding the 0.7% impact from the end of the Venetian agreement.
  • The Ruby acquisition is expected to generate $8 million in incremental fee revenue by 2028, with franchise fees anticipated to exceed $15 million annually by 2030.
  • Future leverage is projected to remain at the lower end of the 2.5x to 3x net debt-to-EBITDA range by the end of 2025.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for 2024 totaled $2.3 billion, with EBIT reaching $1.124 billion, reflecting a 10% year-over-year increase.
  • RevPAR grew 3% for the year, with Q4 achieving 4.6%, driven by a combination of rate and occupancy gains.
  • Operating profit from reportable segments rose 10%, supported by positive trading performance and fee margin progression.

Q&A

  • Jamie Rollo, Morgan Stanley: Questioned the impact of higher key money and its revenue implications. CFO Glover clarified that the increase in key money, driven by Luxury & Lifestyle openings and conversions, would minimally impact fees, with a $5 million to $6 million effect expected in 2025.
  • Vicki Stern, Barclays: Asked about the competitive environment for key money and fee growth in China. CEO Maalouf emphasized that IHG maintains stable fee structures in China and expects high single-digit net unit growth in the region.
  • Jaina Mistry, Jefferies: Inquired about the profitability timeline for Ruby. CEO Maalouf indicated that integration costs in 2025 would prevent immediate accretion, but fee contributions are expected from 2026 onward.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts generally adopted a neutral tone, focusing on key money increases, RevPAR growth potential, and regional performance.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, highlighting achievements in system growth and shareholder returns. The Q&A session featured detailed responses, emphasizing strategic clarity.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent tone, reiterating confidence in long-term growth plans.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, reaffirming over 4% net system growth targets for 2025.
  • Strategic shifts included the Ruby acquisition and the launch of a $900 million share buyback program for 2025, compared to the previous $800 million program in 2024.
  • Management sentiment was steady, with continued emphasis on RevPAR growth and expansion in high-growth geographies.
  • Analysts’ tone remained focused on regional performance metrics, particularly China and the Americas.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged elevated key money levels, attributing the increase to strategic investments in Luxury & Lifestyle segments and conversions.
  • Dependency on global economic stability and geopolitical factors, particularly in regions like China and the Middle East, were noted as potential challenges.
  • Analysts expressed concerns regarding fee growth consistency in the Americas and regional impacts from outbound Chinese travel on ADRs.

Final Takeaway

IHG delivered robust financial performance in 2024, driven by significant system growth and strategic advancements such as the Ruby acquisition. With a $900 million share buyback program and over 4% net system growth targeted for 2025, the company reinforced its commitment to shareholder value. Expansion in premium urban lifestyle and high-growth geographies underpins confidence in sustained long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Franklin Electric targets $2.09B-$2.15B revenue in 2025 with strategic acquisitions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Franklin Electric Co., Inc. (NASDAQ:FELE) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Joseph Ruzynski highlighted that Franklin Electric concluded a challenging year with solid performance. The company focused on restructuring efforts and expanding into faster-growing markets. International business growth, particularly in Europe, Latin America, and APAC, was emphasized as a significant contributor.
  • Ruzynski announced the rebranding of the Fueling Systems segment to Energy Systems, reflecting a broader product portfolio that includes energy-related offerings now comprising 25% of the segment’s revenues. He noted strong U.S. sales and operational efficiencies within this segment.
  • Ruzynski revealed two strategic acquisitions: a water systems business in Australia focused on submersible pumps for mining and industrial sectors, and Barnes de Colombia, which is expected to close in early March 2025. These acquisitions aim to strengthen regional market presence.
  • CFO Jeffery Taylor stated, "Our fully diluted earnings per share were $0.72 for the fourth quarter 2024 versus $0.82 for the fourth quarter 2023," achieving results at the high end of the guidance range.

Outlook

  • Franklin Electric provided full-year 2025 revenue guidance of $2.09B to $2.15B and GAAP EPS guidance between $4.05 and $4.25.
  • Management noted the potential impact of terminating its primary U.S. pension plan during the year, but this is not reflected in the current guidance.
  • Ruzynski expressed optimism about 2025, citing strong order trends, positive book-to-bill ratios, and opportunities in faster-growing verticals.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 consolidated sales were $485.7M, a year-over-year increase of 3%, driven by higher volumes across all three segments and acquisitions, offset by foreign currency headwinds.
  • Gross profit for the quarter reached $164.2M, with a gross margin of 33.8%, consistent with the prior year. Operating income was $43.0M, reflecting a decline from $50.8M in Q4 2023 due to higher SG&A and restructuring expenses.
  • Energy Systems achieved a record operating margin of 35.9%, supported by improved manufacturing productivity and disciplined cost management. Sales in the segment increased 5% year-over-year to $68.8M.
  • The Distribution segment's sales grew 6% to $157.2M, driven by higher volumes and acquisitions, though operating margin declined due to seasonality and commodity pricing pressures.
  • Water Systems segment sales were flat, with growth in U.S. groundwater and international markets offset by foreign currency impacts and a 36% decline in large dewatering equipment sales.

Q&A

  • Matt Summerville, D.A. Davidson: Asked about residential and agricultural groundwater markets for 2025. CEO Ruzynski stated that the residential market, driven by replacement demand, is expected to remain stable, while agricultural demand is less predictable due to pricing challenges.
  • Bryan Blair, Oppenheimer: Inquired about Energy Systems growth rates and margins. CFO Taylor noted that while 2024 faced tough comparisons, demand stabilized in Q4, and margins for critical asset monitoring products are strong.
  • Michael Halloran, Baird: Questioned growth composition and sequential trends for 2025. CFO Taylor projected organic growth of 1%-4% and stated that foreign exchange would remain a $15M-$20M headwind.
  • Walter Liptak, Seaport Global: Asked about the restructuring program. CFO Taylor outlined that restructuring efforts focus on lowering discretionary costs, optimizing footprints, and achieving structural improvements, with projected charges of $3M-$5M.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism but raised concerns about lingering economic headwinds, including housing market challenges, FX impacts, and SG&A expenses. Questions reflected interest in specific growth drivers and cost management.
  • Management maintained a balanced tone, emphasizing resilience and strategic focus. Ruzynski's remarks conveyed confidence in growth through acquisitions and operational efficiencies.
  • Compared to Q3, management's tone reflected greater confidence in future order trends and the benefits of recent restructuring efforts.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 guidance was met, contrasting with the reduced outlook provided in Q3 2024. Management emphasized strategic acquisitions and rebranding efforts this quarter.
  • While Q3 focused on macroeconomic challenges and SG&A cost normalization, Q4 highlighted growth opportunities in Energy Systems and international markets.
  • Analyst sentiment in Q4 was more focused on strategic execution and less on immediate macroeconomic pressures compared to Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited high interest rates, FX headwinds, and housing market pressures as ongoing challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about restructuring execution, SG&A cost containment, and the impact of commodity pricing pressures.
  • Management outlined mitigation strategies, including productivity improvements, cost management, and regional market expansion.

Final Takeaway

Franklin Electric closed 2024 with moderate growth and robust strategic initiatives, including two acquisitions and the rebranding of its Energy Systems segment. The company remains optimistic for 2025, supported by strong order trends, operational efficiencies, and growth in key international markets. However, ongoing pressures from high interest rates, FX impacts, and SG&A expenses will require disciplined execution to maintain positive momentum.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Moderna rises 8% even as Barclays downgrades
data.description:
• Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) is moving bullishly Tuesday with shares up ~8%, despite a downgrade from Barclays.
  • The investment bank cut the biotech to equal weight from overweight, citing a lack of clinical catalysts.
  • Barclays also cut its price target to $45 from $111 (~36% upside based on Feb. 14 close).
  • Analyst Gena Wang noted that despite the disclosure that a phase 3 trial for a norovirus vaccine candidate is on clinical hold due to a single case of Guillain-Barré syndrome, it may not impact timelines for data release given much of the enrollment is complete.
  • "While we continue to believe in MRNA's differentiated RNA platform, we see limited upside to the stock in near term, given macro-environment uncertainties and vaccine policy risk in the US..." she wrote.
  • Moderna reported 2024 Q4 and full-year results on Feb. 14, with a bottom-line miss in the quarter. Today's rise could be due to comments during the earnings call in which management said it would prioritize 10 high-value programs with possible approval over the next three years that are geared toward non-COVID treatments.

data.title: Otter Tail outlines $1.4B capital plan with 9% rate base CAGR through 2029
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Otter Tail Corporation (NASDAQ:OTTR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Otter Tail Corporation reported record earnings for 2024, with diluted earnings per share of $7.17. The company highlighted a significant update to its 5-year capital spending plan, now totaling $1.4 billion for Otter Tail Power, reflecting a 9% rate base compound annual growth rate (OTC:CAGR).
  • CEO Chuck MacFarlane emphasized the company's updated long-term EPS growth target of 6% to 8%, up from the previous 5% to 7%. He also mentioned ongoing progress in wind repowering projects and plans to add up to 345 megawatts of solar generation, contingent on regulatory approvals.
  • Otter Tail Power secured approval for its North Dakota general rate case, achieving a net annual revenue increase of $13.1 million with a return on equity of 10.1%.
  • CFO Todd Wahlund remarked, "We delivered record earnings with diluted EPS totaling $7.17 compared to $7.00 in 2023," and affirmed the company’s strong financial position with total liquidity of $606 million.

Outlook

  • The company initiated its 2025 diluted EPS guidance in the range of $5.68 to $6.08, with an expected return on equity near 14%. Management anticipates Electric segment earnings growth of approximately 7%, offset by declines in the Plastics and Manufacturing segments.
  • Otter Tail Power's 5-year plan forecasts 95% of capital spending to be recovered through existing rates or riders, minimizing regulatory lag and ensuring timely investment recovery. The long-term earnings mix target remains 65% electric and 35% manufacturing, expected by 2028.
  • The company projects Plastics segment earnings to normalize between $45 million and $50 million annually by 2028, delayed from the initial 2026 timeline.

Financial Results

  • The Electric segment saw earnings growth of nearly 8% year-over-year, driven by higher rider revenues, an interim rate increase in North Dakota, and increased commercial and industrial sales volumes.
  • The Plastics segment achieved record annual EPS of $4.77, reflecting a 27% increase in sales volumes due to improved end-market demand. However, sales prices declined by 12% year-over-year.
  • Manufacturing segment earnings faced challenges, with a 15% drop in sales volumes attributed to higher inventory levels, inflationary pressures, and rising interest rates.
  • The company announced a 12% dividend increase, raising the annual indicated dividend for 2025 to $2.10 per share.

Q&A

  • Tate Sullivan, Maxim Group: Questioned progress on large load agreements and their inclusion in capital guidance. CEO Chuck MacFarlane responded that while no electric service agreements have been signed, discussions are underway, and these are not yet included in the capital spending plan.
  • Tate Sullivan, Maxim Group: Asked about manufacturing segment weakness. CEO MacFarlane noted that recreational vehicle, agriculture, and construction industries are facing headwinds, with some sectors correcting inventory levels.
  • Tate Sullivan, Maxim Group: Inquired about the dynamics of large diameter pipe production in Arizona. CFO Todd Wahlund explained that the new capacity in Arizona should boost sales volumes, reduce shipping costs, and improve margins.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a neutral tone, focusing on concerns about the timing and impact of large load agreements and the normalization of Plastics segment earnings. Questions also highlighted macroeconomic pressures on the Manufacturing segment.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks but appeared cautiously optimistic during the Q&A. CEO MacFarlane expressed hope for converting rate base growth into earnings but emphasized ongoing challenges in manufacturing and plastics.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained a similar level of skepticism, while management’s tone remained consistent, focusing on long-term growth prospects and strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The updated 5-year capital spending plan increased to $1.4 billion, up 9% from the previous quarter’s guidance.
  • Guidance for 2025 EPS decreased from the midpoint of $7.07 in Q3 to $5.88 in Q4, reflecting anticipated declines in Plastics segment earnings.
  • Management reaffirmed the delay in Plastics segment normalization, now expected by 2028 instead of 2026, citing declining sales prices as a key factor.
  • Analysts maintained their focus on large load agreements and the impact of macroeconomic conditions, similar to Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing challenges in the Manufacturing segment due to inflation, interest rates, and end-market demand softness.
  • The Plastics segment faces continued declines in sales prices, with potential pressures from increased competition and raw material costs.
  • Regulatory approval dependencies for large solar and transmission projects could impact capital investment timelines.

Final Takeaway

Otter Tail Corporation concluded 2024 with record earnings and significant updates to its long-term growth strategies, including a $1.4 billion capital plan and increased long-term EPS growth targets. While the Electric segment continues to drive strong performance, the Manufacturing and Plastics segments face near-term headwinds. The company remains focused on executing its rate base growth strategy and maintaining a strong financial position, supported by robust liquidity and no equity needs for the next five years.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Merck wins EU nod for Welireg against two kidney cancer indications
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) announced Tuesday that the European Commission approved its anti-cancer agent Welireg (belzutifan) as a treatment for two tumor conditions linked to kidneys.

Accordingly, the oral therapy will be indicated in 27 EU member states and three other countries in the European Economic Area (EEA) as a single agent for adults with von Hippel-Lindau disease and clear cell renal cell carcinoma (RCC).

The approvals are backed by Merck’s (NYSE:MRK) LITESPARK-004 and LITESPARK-005 trials, respectively. An expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency, endorsed the approvals in December by issuing positive opinions.

VHL disease, a rare genetic disorder characterized by blood vessel tumors, frequently leads to RCC, a common form of kidney cancer. An estimated 10,000–15,000 Europeans suffer from VHL disease.

Despite the approval, the company said Welireg's commercial launch in different EU states will depend on the timing of national reimbursements and other factors.
